# figureflow

> A pip-installable anywidget library that renders interactive flow diagrams in any
> Jupyter host or a plain browser tab. An LLM authors a diagram coordinate-free —
> emit topology as JSON or mermaid; figureflow validates, places, and renders it.

This file is the machine-readable summary. The authoritative JSON Schema ships in
the wheel at `figureflow/static/figureflow.schema.json`.

## Authoring a diagram

Two input formats, both coordinate-free (omit positions — the renderer auto-lays
nodes on mount using `layout_direction`):

1. **JSON** — the `figureflow/1` envelope (see the schema file). Minimal example:

   ```json
   {
     "schema": "figureflow/1",
     "layout_direction": "TB",
     "nodes": [
       {"id": "a", "data": {"label": "Start", "shape": "stadium"}},
       {"id": "b", "data": {"label": "Decide", "shape": "diamond"}}
     ],
     "edges": [{"source": "a", "target": "b", "label": "go"}]
   }
   ```

   - Node shapes (`data.shape`): rectangle, rounded, stadium, ellipse, diamond,
     parallelogram, hexagon, cylinder.
   - `position` is optional. Omit it; do not invent coordinates.
   - `layout_direction`: TB (default), BT, LR, RL.

2. **mermaid flowchart** — the bounded subset figureflow imports:

   | Construct | Forms |
   | --- | --- |
   | Header | `flowchart <dir>` / `graph <dir>`; dir `TB TD BT LR RL` (TD→TB; missing→TB) |
   | Node shapes | `id[rect]` `id(rounded)` `id([stadium])` `id((ellipse))` `id{diamond}` `id{{hexagon}}` `id[/parallelogram/]` `id[(cylinder)]`; bare `id` |
   | Labels | unquoted, `"quoted"`, `<br>`→newline; first definition wins |
   | Edge ops | `-->` `---` `-.->` `-.-` `==>` `===` |
   | Edge labels | `-->|label|` and `-- label -->` |
   | Chains & fans | `a --> b --> c`; `a & b --> c` |
   | Subgraph | `subgraph id [title] … end`, one level → a group |
   | Comments | `%%` to end of line |

   Other diagram types (sequence, class, state, gantt) are not imported.
   `classDef`/`class`/`style`/`linkStyle`/`click` are warn-and-skipped (style
   fidelity is out of scope — figureflow styling is per-element Python kwargs).

## Error-message contract (the repair loop)

`from_json` / `from_mermaid` collect **every** problem and raise one error,
one problem per line:

```
<json-path>: <what is wrong>. <hint with valid alternatives>
```

e.g. `nodes[3].shape: 'circle' is not a valid shape. Use one of: rectangle,
rounded, stadium, ellipse, diamond, parallelogram, hexagon, cylinder.` Paste the
error back to fix all faults in one round-trip. `strict=True` escalates forgiving
coercions/skips to errors.

## MCP tools (`pip install 'figureflow[mcp]'`, console script `figureflow-mcp`)

One live diagram per server process; the agent and a human edit the same canvas.

- `create_diagram(source, format="json"|"mermaid", open_browser=True) -> {url, warnings}`
- `get_diagram() -> {json, mermaid}` — current state, including human edits
- `replace_diagram(source, format) -> {warnings}` — wholesale reload
- `add_elements(nodes_json="[]", edges_json="[]") -> {warnings}` — incremental add
- `close_diagram() -> {}`

Validation/parse faults are returned as `{error: "<collected messages>"}` tool
results (never protocol errors), so the repair loop reads them as content.

## Python API

```python
from figureflow import Flow
flow = Flow.from_json(envelope_str)        # or Flow.from_mermaid(mermaid_str)
flow.serve()                                # live browser tab
flow.to_html("out.html")                    # offline interactive snapshot
print(flow.to_json(), flow.to_mermaid())    # export
```
